requirements.title: Module Root Structure
requirements.description: The module system shall define a module root struct containing an API vtable pointer.
requirements.rationale: The module root struct is the foundation of the vtable-based dependency injection system, enabling polymorphic dispatch across all LibJuno modules.

requirements.title: Module Root Failure Handler
requirements.description: The module root shall contain a failure handler pointer and user data pointer.
requirements.rationale: Provides a diagnostic callback mechanism so integrators can observe failures without altering control flow, which is essential for debugging on targets with limited instrumentation.

requirements.title: Module Derivation
requirements.description: Derived module structs shall embed the root as their first member, enabling safe upcasting to the root type.
requirements.rationale: Struct embedding with the root as the first member enables safe upcasting in C, providing polymorphism without dynamic allocation or language extensions.

requirements.title: Module Union
requirements.description: A module union shall contain the root and all derivation types for polymorphic storage.
requirements.rationale: The union enables caller-allocated polymorphic storage that is correctly sized for any derivation, without requiring the caller to know which concrete type will be used at compile time.

requirements.title: Result Type
requirements.description: The module system shall define a result type combining a status code with a typed success payload.
requirements.rationale: Combines a status code with a typed payload in a single return value, enabling functions to communicate both success data and error conditions without out-parameters or global state.

requirements.title: Option Type
requirements.description: The module system shall define an option type combining a presence flag with a typed payload.
requirements.rationale: Provides explicit representation of optional values, eliminating sentinel-value conventions and making the presence or absence of a value type-safe and self-documenting.
